excerpt: The Human Rights Watch Anfal report summary states that between 3,200 and 5,000 residents died in the March 16 bombing of Halabja.
transform_note: Used as the core range because it is explicitly tied to the bounded March 16 bombing rather than later sequelae.
confidence_note: Moderate-to-high confidence for a direct-death range, but not for a status split.

excerpt: The U.S. State Department statement says that an estimated 5,000 civilians were killed when the Iraqi military attacked Halabja with chemical weapons on 16 March 1988.
transform_note: Preserved as a source-specific civilian claim but not used to reallocate the full episode total into a civilian-only estimate.
confidence_note: Moderate confidence as a common official figure; low confidence for precise status coding without underlying documentation.
